phrase: Rent is due on the first.
example: Rent is due on the first — don't forget or you'll get hit with a late fee.

phrase: When is trash day?
example: When is trash day? I want to make sure I put the bin out in time.

phrase: Put the trash out tonight.
example: Put the trash out tonight — they come around six in the morning.

phrase: The AC is not working.
example: The AC is not working and it's a hundred degrees outside — I need to call the landlord.

phrase: I need to submit a maintenance request.
pronunciation: /aɪ nid tə səbˈmɪt ə ˈmeɪntənəns rɪˈkwɛst/
example: I need to submit a maintenance request — the kitchen faucet has been leaking for days.

phrase: The landlord needs to fix this.
example: The landlord needs to fix this — it's been broken since I moved in.

phrase: My lease is up next month.
example: My lease is up next month — I need to decide whether to renew or move.

phrase: I'm thinking about renewing my lease.
example: I'm thinking about renewing my lease — the location is really convenient.

phrase: I'm looking for a new apartment.
example: I'm looking for a new apartment closer to work — nothing fancy, just affordable.

phrase: Is utilities included?
example: Is utilities included in the rent, or do I pay that separately?

phrase: The water bill is higher than usual.
pronunciation: /ðə ˈwɔtər bɪl ɪz ˈhaɪər ðæn ˈjuʒuəl/
example: The water bill is higher than usual — I think there might be a leak somewhere.

phrase: My electricity bill is through the roof.
pronunciation: /maɪ ɪˌlɛkˈtrɪsɪti bɪl ɪz θru ðə ruf/
example: My electricity bill is through the roof — running the AC all summer is brutal.

phrase: I need to do laundry today.
example: I need to do laundry today — I'm completely out of clean clothes.

phrase: The washer is acting up.
example: The washer is acting up again — it keeps stopping mid-cycle.

phrase: Can you take out the recycling?
example: Can you take out the recycling? The bin is overflowing.

phrase: I need to vacuum the living room.
pronunciation: /aɪ nid tə ˈvækjuəm ðə ˈlɪvɪŋ rum/
example: I need to vacuum the living room — there's dog hair everywhere.

phrase: The sink is clogged.
example: The sink is clogged again — do we have any drain cleaner?

phrase: The toilet keeps running.
example: The toilet keeps running — I think the flapper needs to be replaced.

phrase: There's a leak under the sink.
example: There's a leak under the sink — everything under there is soaking wet.

phrase: Can you help me move this furniture?
pronunciation: /kæn ju hɛlp mi muv ðɪs ˈfɜrnɪtʃər/
example: Can you help me move this furniture? I want to rearrange the living room.

phrase: I need to deep clean the bathroom.
pronunciation: /aɪ nid tə dip klin ðə ˈbæθˌrum/
example: I need to deep clean the bathroom this weekend — it's overdue.

phrase: Let's split the chores.
example: Let's split the chores — you do the dishes and I'll sweep the floors.

phrase: Who's turn is it to do the dishes?
pronunciation: /huz tɜrn ɪz ɪt tə du ðə ˈdɪʃɪz/
example: Whose turn is it to do the dishes? I did them last night.

phrase: The dishwasher is full — can you run it?
pronunciation: /ðə ˈdɪʃˌwɑʃər ɪz fʊl kæn ju rʌn ɪt/
example: The dishwasher is full — can you run it before you go to bed?

phrase: Don't forget to lock the door.
example: Don't forget to lock the door — there's been some activity in the neighborhood.

phrase: The porch light burned out.
example: The porch light burned out — can you grab a bulb from the closet?

phrase: I need to mow the lawn.
example: I need to mow the lawn — it's getting embarrassingly tall.

phrase: The yard needs some work.
example: The yard needs some work — especially after all that rain last week.

phrase: I need to water the plants.
example: I need to water the plants — this Texas heat dries them out fast.

phrase: The filter needs to be changed.
example: The filter needs to be changed — the air quality has been terrible lately.

phrase: I need pest control.
example: I need pest control — I found ants in the kitchen again this morning.

phrase: There are cockroaches in the apartment.
example: There are cockroaches in the apartment — that's a landlord issue, not mine.

phrase: The roof is leaking.
example: The roof is leaking every time it rains — I've reported it twice already.

phrase: The smoke detector went off.
example: The smoke detector went off — I was just cooking, nothing to worry about.

phrase: The battery in the detector is dead.
pronunciation: /ðə ˈbætəri ɪn ðə dɪˈtɛktər ɪz dɛd/
example: The battery in the detector is dead — that's what that chirping sound is.

phrase: I'm going to repaint the walls.
example: I'm going to repaint the walls before I move out — I want my deposit back.

phrase: I'm trying to get my deposit back.
pronunciation: /aɪm ˈtraɪɪŋ tə ɡɛt maɪ dɪˈpɑzɪt bæk/
example: I'm trying to get my deposit back — I cleaned everything top to bottom.

phrase: The neighbors are too loud.
example: The neighbors are too loud — it's midnight and they're still playing music.

phrase: Can you keep it down?
example: Can you keep it down? The baby just fell asleep.

phrase: There's mold in the bathroom.
example: There's mold in the bathroom — it's a health hazard and needs to be addressed.

phrase: The heater isn't working.
example: The heater isn't working — the one cold week in Texas and this happens.

phrase: I need to call a plumber.
example: I need to call a plumber — the pipe under the sink burst this morning.

phrase: Can I borrow your tools?
example: Can I borrow your tools? I need a drill to hang some shelves.

phrase: I'm handy around the house.
example: I'm pretty handy around the house — I'd rather fix things myself than call someone.

phrase: The garbage disposal is broken.
example: The garbage disposal is broken — it's just humming and not spinning anymore.

phrase: I need to organize the garage.
example: I need to organize the garage — there's no room to even park in there anymore.

phrase: Time to declutter.
example: Time to declutter — if I haven't used it in a year, it's going to Goodwill.

phrase: Scrub the grout in the shower.
example: Scrub the grout in the shower — it's been discolored for way too long.

phrase: Sweep the floors before you mop.
example: Sweep the floors before you mop — otherwise you're just spreading the dirt around.

phrase: Empty the dryer lint trap.
example: Empty the dryer lint trap every time — it's a fire hazard if you don't.

phrase: The window won't close all the way.
pronunciation: /ðə ˈwɪndoʊ woʊnt kloʊz ɔl ðə weɪ/
example: The window won't close all the way — the bugs are getting in at night.

phrase: The landlord is coming to inspect.
example: The landlord is coming to inspect next week — I need to clean everything.

phrase: How much is the security deposit?
example: How much is the security deposit? And is it refundable when I move out?

phrase: The apartment is pet-friendly.
example: The apartment is pet-friendly — there's a small fee for dogs though.

phrase: I need to call the super.
example: I need to call the super — the hallway light has been out for a week.

phrase: The dishwasher is making a weird noise.
pronunciation: /ðə ˈdɪʃˌwɑʃər ɪz ˈmeɪkɪŋ ə wɪrd nɔɪz/
example: The dishwasher is making a weird noise — I think something is caught in the drain.

phrase: I need to scrub the grout.
example: I need to scrub the grout — the bathroom tile is looking pretty dingy.

phrase: Is recycling picked up on the same day?
pronunciation: /ɪz rɪˈsaɪklɪŋ pɪkt ʌp ɑn ðə seɪm deɪ/
example: Is recycling picked up on the same day as trash, or a different day?

phrase: Let's do a deep clean this weekend.
pronunciation: /lɛts du ə dip klin ðɪs ˈwikˌɛnd/
example: Let's do a deep clean this weekend — the whole place needs a proper scrub down.

phrase: The garbage bags are under the sink.
pronunciation: /ðə ˈɡɑrbɪdʒ bæɡz ər ˈʌndər ðə sɪŋk/
example: The garbage bags are under the sink — grab one and take the trash out please.

phrase: Can you fix the loose cabinet door?
pronunciation: /kæn ju fɪks ðə lus ˈkæbɪnɪt dɔr/
example: Can you fix the loose cabinet door? It keeps swinging open on its own.

phrase: I need to patch a hole in the wall.
pronunciation: /aɪ nid tə pætʃ ə hoʊl ɪn ðə wɔl/
example: I need to patch a hole in the wall before I move out — I want my deposit back.

phrase: The hot water isn't working.
example: The hot water isn't working again — I had to take a cold shower this morning.

phrase: I need to caulk the shower.
example: I need to caulk the shower — the old stuff is peeling and water is getting behind the tile.
